sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: How Much Is the 2026 Mitsubishi Outlander?
feed.summary: The 2026 Mitsubishi Outlander introduces a new standard turbocharged hybrid engine, starting at $31,955. While horsepower slightly decreases, torque sees a significant gain, improving in-town drivability. New trim levels, including the LE, SEL Black Edition, and Ralliart, will be released later in 2026.

feed.title: Why AXT (AXTI) Is Down 8.3% After Indium Phosphide Export Constraints and Capacity Expansion Plans - And What's Next
feed.summary: AXT, Inc. recently announced updated Q4 2025 revenue expectations and discussed challenges with Chinese export permits for indium phosphide, which limited shipments despite strong demand. The company plans to significantly expand its indium phosphide capacity by H2 2026, funded by a December capital raise, to capitalize on AI-related data center infrastructure and high-speed optical connectivity. This strategy aims to address regulatory bottlenecks and leverage growing demand, while analysts have varying fair value estimates for the stock.

feed.title: F5, Inc. Securities Class Action Deadline Approaches Amid Significant Stock Drop
feed.summary: F5, Inc. shareholders are reminded that the deadline to file lead plaintiff applications in a securities class action lawsuit is February 17, 2026. This lawsuit follows a significant 10.9% stock drop after F5's October 27, 2025 earnings report revealed below-market growth expectations for fiscal 2026. The legal action alleges that F5 and certain executives failed to disclose material information, violating federal securities laws, with ClaimsFiler offering free legal consultation for affected investors.

feed.title: MITSUI SUMITOMO INSURANCE CO LTD Increases Stake in WR Berkley C
feed.summary: MITSUI SUMITOMO INSURANCE CO LTD, a 10% owner of WR Berkley Corp (WRB), significantly increased its stake by purchasing 811,041 shares on January 14, 2026, bringing its total holdings to over 52.7 million shares. This aligns with a trend of insider buying at WR Berkley, with six insider buys and zero sells in the past year. At the time of purchase, WR Berkley's stock traded at $68.58, and GuruFocus estimates the stock to be fairly valued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.02.

feed.title: CoStar Group (NASDAQ:CSGP) Shares Acquired Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr.
feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. (D-California) recently purchased between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of CoStar Group (NASDAQ:CSGP) stock on December 10th, disclosed in a January 12th filing. CoStar Group's stock traded up 3.3% recently, following its approval of a $1.5 billion share buyback after reporting strong quarterly results and setting positive FY2025 guidance. While analysts have mixed opinions, the consensus rating for CSGP is a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $82.50.

feed.title: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. Sells Global Payments Inc. (NYSE:GPN) Stock
feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. recently sold between $15,001 and $50,000 worth of Global Payments Inc. (NYSE:GPN) stock on December 10th. Global Payments reported strong earnings, exceeding analyst estimates, and has a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with a target price of $99.30.

feed.title: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. Purchases Shares of The Cooper Companies, Inc. (NASDAQ:COO)
feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. (D-California) recently purchased between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of shares in The Cooper Companies, Inc. (NASDAQ:COO). Cooper Companies recently exceeded quarterly EPS estimates, provided positive FY2026 guidance, and authorized a $2.0 billion share repurchase program, leading analysts to rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy."

feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. recently sold between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of shares in The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE:SCHW) on December 10th from his "150 MAIN STREET TRUST > BANK OF AMERICA" account. Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) shares were up 0.7% to $101.88, having recently reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results with an EPS of $1.31 and revenue of $6.38 billion. The company offers a quarterly dividend of $0.27 and has a consensus "Moderate Buy" analyst rating with a target price of $112.24.

feed.title: Sen. John Boozman Purchases Shares of Arista Networks, Inc. (NYSE:ANET)
feed.summary: Senator John Boozman (R-Arkansas) disclosed a purchase of Arista Networks (ANET) shares valued between $1,001 and $15,000 on December 11th, 2025. This comes as Arista Networks shares recently traded down, despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts with an average target price of $164.44. feed.title: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. Buys Stryker Corporation (NYSE:SYK) Shares
feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. (D-California) recently purchased between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of Stryker Corporation (NYSE:SYK) stock on December 10th. This purchase occurred despite company insiders selling a significant number of shares in the last 90 days. Stryker recently reported strong quarterly earnings, exceeding estimates and increasing its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: CHF129.00 3M Co (MMM.SW) SIX pre-market Jan 2026 earnings spotlight: guidance key
feed.summary: 3M Co (MMM.SW) is trading at CHF129.00 in pre-market ahead of its Q4 2025 earnings report on January 20, 2026. Investors are focusing on management's guidance for organic growth and margins across its four segments, as well as updates on free cash flow and liabilities. Meyka AI rates MMM.SW with a "HOLD" suggestion and projects a yearly upside of 12.76% from the current price.

feed.title: Grocery Outlet’s chief purchasing officer is retiring after 31 years
feed.summary: Grocery Outlet Holding Corp. announced the retirement of Steve Wilson, EVP and Chief Purchasing Officer, after 31 years with the company, effective March 20. Matt Delly, Chief Merchandising Officer, will assume expanded responsibilities as Chief Merchandising and Purchasing Officer. This restructuring aims to improve operational efficiency, following a lowered forecast for fourth-quarter sales growth due to a delay in food assistance benefits during a government shutdown.

feed.title: Comfort Systems (FIX) Declines More Than Market: Some Information for Investors
feed.summary: Comfort Systems (FIX) stock declined by 1.87% in the most recent trading session, underperforming the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq. Despite the recent dip, the company has seen a 10.8% gain over the past month. Investors are awaiting its upcoming earnings report, with forecasts suggesting significant EPS and revenue growth.

feed.title: Scotiabank Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Brixmor Property Group (NYSE:BRX) Stock Price
feed.summary: Scotiabank has lowered its price objective for Brixmor Property Group (NYSE:BRX) from $30.00 to $29.00 but maintained a "sector outperform" rating, indicating a potential 12.51% upside. Despite this slight reduction, the overall analyst sentiment remains positive with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average target price of $30.30. Brixmor reported in-line Q4 EPS of $0.56, revenue up 6.3% year-over-year, and issued FY2025 EPS guidance of $2.23-$2.25.

feed.title: H.B. Fuller beats Q4 earnings estimates as profit margins expand
feed.summary: H.B. Fuller (NYSE:FUL) reported adjusted Q4 EPS of $1.28, beating analyst estimates, despite revenue of $894.8 million falling slightly short of expectations. The company demonstrated significant profitability improvement with adjusted EBITDA up 14.6% year-over-year, driven by margin expansion to 19.0%. For fiscal 2026, H.B. Fuller forecasts flat to 2% revenue growth and adjusted EPS between $4.35 and $4.70.

feed.title: Assessing KeyCorp (KEY) Valuation As Shares Trade Near Estimated Fair Value And Peer P/E Premium
feed.summary: KeyCorp (KEY) is currently trading near its estimated fair value of US$22.80, with its shares at US$20.95, indicating it might be 8.1% undervalued according to a popular narrative. Despite positive revenue and net income growth and strong recent share price performance, its P/E ratio of 24.4x is significantly higher than peers (14x) and the broader US Banks group (11.9x), suggesting the market might already be pricing in future growth and offering less of a safety cushion. Investors are encouraged to consider potential risks like weakening asset quality or pressures on earnings, and to conduct their own analysis.

feed.title: Is Analyst Optimism Revealing the Real Strength of Weyerhaeuser's (WY) Timberland-Centric Model?
feed.summary: Analysts have recently reaffirmed positive views on Weyerhaeuser's business model, highlighting its timberland assets, carbon credits, and recreation income, alongside low leverage and portfolio optimization. This optimism, driven by the scarcity of timberland and expected improvement in funds from operations, suggests Weyerhaeuser's diverse model could mitigate sector challenges despite softer lumber demand and housing activity. The company's investment narrative focuses on the long-term value of its timberland and its ability to generate consistent cash flows, with a projected fair value of $29.82.

feed.title: Salesforce says AI agent adoption boosts holiday sales growth 59% - CHOSUNBIZ
feed.summary: Salesforce has announced that the adoption of AI agents significantly boosted holiday sales growth for its users. The company reported a substantial 59% increase in sales attributed to the use of these AI-powered tools during the holiday period. This indicates a strong positive impact of artificial intelligence on retail performance.

feed.title: A Look At Welltower (WELL) Valuation As Optimistic Q4 Earnings Expectations Fuel Bullish Analyst Sentiment
feed.summary: Welltower (WELL) is approaching its Q4 earnings release with optimistic expectations, including a projected double-digit EPS increase and a history of exceeding profit forecasts. While a common narrative suggests the stock is 9% undervalued with a fair value of $207, its current P/E ratio of 134.9x indicates investors are paying a significant premium compared to industry averages and its own fair ratio, raising questions about valuation risk if growth slows.

feed.title: Is Pool Corp (POOL) Now At An Attractive Price After Recent Share Rebound
feed.summary: Pool Corp (POOL) has experienced a recent rebound in its share price, with short-term gains offsetting longer-term declines. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 12.0%, while a Price/Earnings (P/E) comparison indicates it is overvalued compared to Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio. The article encourages investors to use personalized narratives to determine the stock's fair value.

feed.title: NextEra Energy Inc.: How America’s Renewables Flagship Became a Blueprint for the Post-Fossil Util
feed.summary: NextEra Energy Inc. is redefining the utility sector by treating clean power as a technology product through its integrated platform of wind, solar, storage, and grid solutions. The company operates two main fronts: Florida Power & Light (FPL), a regulated utility, and NextEra Energy Resources, its global renewables arm. This dual identity allows NextEra to lead in decarbonizing the U.S. power grid while maintaining predictable returns and reliability, showcasing how the energy transition can be investable at scale.

feed.title: M&T Bank (MTB) Reports Q4: Everything You Need To Know Ahead Of Earnings
feed.summary: M&T Bank (MTB) is set to report its Q4 earnings this Friday before market open. Analysts expect the company's revenue to grow 4.2% year-on-year to $2.48 billion, with adjusted earnings projected at $4.47 per share. The company has a mixed track record with revenue estimates, missing three times in the last two years, but shares are up 2.6% over the last month and average analyst price target is $227.70.

feed.title: Intuit (INTU) Shares Hit Worst Drop Since August 2024, Retail Investors Bullish on Rebound
feed.summary: Intuit (INTU) shares experienced their largest drop since August 2024, falling 6.4% and reaching a nine-month low. Despite this decline and market concerns, retail investors are showing extreme bullish sentiment, viewing the current price as a buying opportunity, which has led to a significant surge in trading volume. Analysts also remain largely optimistic, with a majority rating Intuit as 'Buy' or higher, projecting a substantial upside from its current price.

feed.title: CarMax lays off more than 200 employees
feed.summary: CarMax announced the layoff of nearly 230 employees, with 113 in the Richmond area, impacting its Home Office and CarMax Auto Finance departments. The company stated the layoffs were a necessary measure to reduce costs and foster a more agile corporate workforce. Affected employees were offered severance, career support, and opportunities to apply for internal positions.

feed.title: Automatic Data Processing (NASDAQ:ADP) Approves Stock Repurchase Program
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing (NASDAQ:ADP) has approved a $6.00 billion share repurchase program, allowing the company to buy back up to 5.8% of its outstanding stock. This move, declared on January 14th, is often seen as a signal that the board believes the stock is undervalued. Additionally, ADP recently raised its quarterly dividend to $1.70 per share, increased its FY2026 earnings per share guidance, and reported strong quarterly results that beat analyst estimates.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities Class Action Notice, Deadline January 26
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm has issued a class action notice for investors who purchased Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) securities between January 27 and October 27, 2025, with a lead plaintiff deadline of January 26. The lawsuit alleges that the company provided misleading positive information while concealing negative facts about its Long Island City property, leading to investor losses. Analysts have varying outlooks on ARE, with an average 1-year price target of $64.78.

feed.title: TD Cowen Trims Strategy Price Target as Bitcoin Yield Outlook Softens
feed.summary: TD Cowen has reduced its price target for Strategy (MSTR) to $440 from $500, despite maintaining a "buy" rating. This adjustment reflects the near-term impact of Strategy's aggressive Bitcoin purchases during a period of price compression. Analysts note that Strategy's approach aligns with broader shifts in Bitcoin's institutional market structure, indicating increased market depth and stable liquidity through traditional financial channels.

feed.title: State Street (STT) Q4 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
feed.summary: State Street (STT) is set to release its Q4 earnings report this Friday. Analysts are projecting a 5.9% year-over-year revenue growth to $3.61 billion and adjusted earnings of $2.84 per share. The company has a strong record of exceeding revenue estimates, and recent positive sentiment in the capital markets segment suggests a favorable outlook.

feed.title: Ardent Health Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over Misrepresentation of Receivables
feed.summary: Ardent Health is facing a class action lawsuit filed by Rosen Law Firm on behalf of investors, alleging misrepresentation of accounts receivable and failure to maintain adequate professional liability insurance. The lawsuit claims Ardent Health made false statements regarding its collectability assessments, leading to investor losses. Despite this, analyst views indicate a potential rise in ARDT stock price over the next 12 months, with an average 1-year price target of $13.96.

feed.title: PNC Financial Services Group (PNC) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: PNC Financial Services Group (PNC) is set to announce its earnings results this Friday. Analysts expect the company's revenue to grow by 6.7% year-on-year to $5.97 billion, with adjusted earnings projected at $4.23 per share. Despite missing revenue estimates twice in the last two years, analysts have generally maintained their estimates, and the stock is trading with an average analyst price target higher than its current share price.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Shareholder Alert: ClaimsFiler
feed.summary: ClaimsFiler is reminding investors who suffered losses exceeding $100,000 in Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (ARE) securities to file lead plaintiff applications by January 26, 2026. This alert concerns a securities class action lawsuit against Alexandria for allegedly failing to disclose material information that led to a significant stock price drop after the company reported lower-than-expected Q3 2025 financial results and a $323.9 million real estate impairment charge. Lawyers at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC are available to discuss legal options for affected investors.

feed.title: Ascendis Pharma grants 42,030 employee warrants at $215.05 per share
feed.summary: Ascendis Pharma (NASDAQ:ASND) has granted 42,030 employee warrants, each allowing employees to subscribe for one ordinary share at an exercise price of $215.05, matching the closing price of its American Depositary Shares on the grant date, January 13, 2026. The warrants will vest over four years, with 25% vesting after one year and the remaining 75% in monthly installments thereafter, contingent on continued service. The company's board can issue up to 1,683,203 additional shares through warrants.

feed.title: Nvidia vs. Alphabet: Which Is the Better AI Growth Stock for 2026?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Nvidia and Alphabet as AI growth stocks for 2026, comparing their business models, financial performance, and valuations. While Nvidia is praised for its explosive growth in AI infrastructure, Alphabet is highlighted for its diversified AI integration across various platforms and expanding Google Cloud profitability. The author ultimately suggests Alphabet as a better long-term investment due to its more diversified and less cyclical business model, despite acknowledging risks associated with both companies.

feed.title: Baxter Unveils Dynamo Smart Stretcher to Improve Hospital Safety
feed.summary: Baxter International Inc. is launching its Dynamo Series smart stretcher in the U.S., enhancing its connected care solutions for hospitals. This new stretcher aims to improve patient safety, comfort, and workflow efficiency by reducing unnecessary transfers and integrating advanced fall prevention technologies. The launch reinforces Baxter's focus on technological innovation in acute care, addressing clinical needs and supporting its Care & Connectivity segment, with the smart hospital beds market projected to grow significantly.

feed.title: Nvidia vs. Alphabet: Which Is the Better AI Growth Stock for 2026?
feed.summary: This article compares Nvidia and Alphabet as AI growth stocks, analyzing their performance and future prospects for 2026. While Nvidia demonstrates impressive growth and dominance in AI chips, its valuation is considered high and its business more cyclical. Alphabet, with its diversified services and rapidly growing Google Cloud, coupled with a significant AI distribution partnership with Apple, is presented as a more sustainable but still somewhat risky investment due to its valuation and regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: Mission Produce to acquire Calavo Growers in $430m avocado industry deal
feed.summary: Mission Produce is set to acquire Calavo Growers in a $430 million cash-and-stock deal, combining Mission's vertically integrated avocado platform with Calavo's long-standing sourcing capabilities. This acquisition aims to create a more diversified company with enhanced scale, expanding its premium avocado position in North America and strengthening its global fresh produce platform. The transaction is expected to generate $25 million in annualised cost synergies within 18 months and reinforces the companies' commitment to operational excellence and customer satisfaction.

feed.title: ORCL Investors Have Opportunity to Join Oracle Corporation Fraud Investigation with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is investigating Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) on behalf of investors for alleged securities law violations. This follows a Motley Fool report indicating Oracle is facing a lawsuit from bondholders who claim the company concealed plans for substantial additional capital raises shortly after an initial $18 billion bond offering, related to a $300 billion OpenAI deal. Oracle's shares fell by almost 4.3% as a result of this news.

feed.title: Assessing Coherent (COHR) Valuation After WaveShaper 1000A Sharp Launch And AI Data Center Momentum
feed.summary: Coherent (COHR) recently launched its WaveShaper 1000A Sharp, an advanced optical filter, amidst strong AI data center and telecom momentum, leading to a 58.77% share price return in 90 days. Despite this growth, Simply Wall St's narrative suggests Coherent is mildly overvalued at $184.11, with a fair value of $178.47, highlighting that recent product news and AI buzz are already priced into the stock. Investors are encouraged to consider the full narrative, including risks like competition and manufacturing investment payoffs, and undertake their own research.

feed.title: American Express stock (AXP) ends flat as Trump’s 10% credit-card cap keeps policy risk in play
feed.summary: American Express (AXP) stock closed flat despite earlier volatility, as President Trump's proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates continues to pressure the financial sector. Other major card issuers like Visa and Mastercard have also seen dips amidst this policy debate, which analysts view as a potential buying opportunity for long-term investors. A new fintech competitor, Bilt, has further complicated the landscape by offering a 10% introductory APR, while larger banks warn of negative economic impacts if the cap is implemented.

feed.title: Hilton Increases Stock Repurchase Authorization
feed.summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. announced that its Board of Directors has authorized a $3.5 billion increase to its existing stock repurchase program, bringing the total authorized amount for future repurchases to approximately $4.6 billion. The company may repurchase shares in the open market or through privately negotiated transactions, with the timing and amount dependent on market conditions and liquidity. The program is flexible and does not obligate Hilton to repurchase any specific amount of shares.

feed.title: Amazon stock posts worst day in 2 months as it falls behind 'Mag 7' peers in the new year
feed.summary: Amazon stock experienced its worst trading day in two months, underperforming its "Magnificent Seven" peers in the new year. This decline indicates a potential shift or challenge in its market performance compared to other major tech stocks.

feed.title: AeroVironment Launches Mission Specialist Wraith UUV
feed.summary: AeroVironment Inc. has launched the Mission Specialist Wraith, a new Unmanned Underwater Vehicle (UUV) designed for demanding subsea operations. This compact UUV offers six-degree-of-freedom agility, high thrust, and precision control, making it suitable for inspection, defense, and scientific missions. The Wraith is built on VideoRay's open-architecture Mission Specialist design, allowing for rapid reconfiguration and integration of third-party tools, with future iterations planned to expand its capabilities.

feed.title: Crescent Biopharma Advances CR-001 Cancer Trial Into First-in-Human Stage
feed.summary: Crescent Biopharma, Inc. (CBIO) has announced the advancement of its CR-001 cancer trial into the first-in-human stage with a Phase 1/2 study. The trial aims to evaluate the safety, pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, and antitumor activity of CR-001 in adults with locally advanced or metastatic solid tumors. While the study is currently in the "Not Yet Recruiting" phase, this marks a significant value inflection point for the biotech firm, potentially influencing future capital raises or partnerships.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on SailPoint: "I Just Do Not Want to Be in Anything Like That"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed strong disinterest in investing in enterprise software companies like SailPoint, likening it to his experience with Salesforce, which he feels constantly underperforms. SailPoint provides identity governance and administration software crucial for cybersecurity in the AI era. While acknowledging its potential, the article suggests other AI stocks might offer greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: 2 ETFs for Getting More Yield and Shortening Duration
feed.summary: This article discusses two Vanguard ETFs, VGIT and VCIT, for bond investors looking for more yield and shorter duration in a rising interest rate environment. VGIT focuses on intermediate-term U.S. Treasury bonds, offering a balance between yield and interest rate risk. VCIT provides exposure to intermediate-term corporate bonds for those willing to accept more credit risk in exchange for higher yields.

feed.title: Is Vulcan Materials (VMC) Among the Best AI Stocks to Buy Now?
feed.summary: The article "Is Vulcan Materials (VMC) Among the Best AI Stocks to Buy Now?" is not available in the provided content. The current content only contains the title and publication time. Therefore, no summary can be generated.

feed.title: Trump Targets Taiwan, China Supply Chains: President Slaps 25% Tariffs On Nvidia, AMD AI Chips Under National Security Order
feed.summary: President Donald Trump has imposed a 25% tariff on select high-end AI chips from companies like Nvidia and AMD, following a nine-month investigation that highlighted U.S. dependence on foreign-made chips as a national security risk. The tariffs are narrowly targeted to encourage domestic chip production and reduce reliance on manufacturing hubs like Taiwan, with exemptions for certain applications. This action follows previous pledges regarding Chinese semiconductor imports and adds complexity to the semiconductor supply chain.

feed.title: Booking Holdings (BKNG) stock slides as visa pause and China probe hit travel shares
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) stock and other travel shares fell due to shifts in U.S. immigration policy and increased regulatory scrutiny. The travel sector is facing challenges from a new U.S. immigrant visa pause and an antitrust probe by China's market regulator into Trip.com, highlighting policy risks tied to cross-border movement. Despite some resilience, investors are concerned about potential crackdowns on online travel platform fees and market dominance, and the impact of anti-immigration policies on global tourism.

feed.title: Ascentage Pharma's Dual-Engine Growth: Global Innovation Strategy for 2026
feed.summary: Ascentage Pharma is poised for significant growth in 2026, driven by its core products, Olverembatinib and Lisaftoclax, and the FDA IND clearance for APG-3288. The company is advancing multiple global Phase III trials and plans to expand its commercial sales to 1,500 hospitals, aiming for Lisaftoclax's inclusion in China's National Reimbursement Drug List. Analyst forecasts indicate a positive outlook for AAPG stock with a projected rise over the next 12 months.

feed.title: Eversource encourages its Connecticut customers to seek help with rising energy bills
feed.summary: Eversource is urging its Connecticut customers to take advantage of available resources to manage increasing energy bills, which tend to rise in winter due to higher natural gas prices. The utility is hosting in-person events to provide information on financial assistance, flexible payment plans, and energy-efficient programs. These initiatives aim to help customers reduce unpaid balances, prevent service disconnections, and lower monthly costs.
